title: Tuzes-Adaz
content:

{{Quote|Something plagues their land. Champion after champion have been sent out, yet none have returned. They were choosing another one when you intervened.|Obi-Wan Kenobi translates Tuzes-Adaz's words for Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="CW23">{{CW|23}}</ref>}}
'''Tuzes-Adaz''' was a [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was the [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of multiple [[Tuzes-Adaz's son|sons]] and who served as the [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] of the [[City/Legends|village]] [[Rokrul]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. [[Time/Legends|Sometime]] during the war, the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] established a [[Techno Union laboratory|facility]] on Nelvaan that drained the planet of geothermal energy.

The resulting environmental shift alarmed Tuzes-Adaz and her fellow Nelvaanians, who blamed the change on an illness plaguing the [[Great Mother]] [[Deity/Legends|deity]]. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the chieftain discovered the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and brought them back to Rokrul to meet the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Orvos]], who had prophesied of an [[Holt Kazed|off-worlder]] who would save their planet. Tuzes-Adaz's choice proved wise, as Skywalker went on to [[Battle of Nelvaan|defeat]] the Techno Union and destroy the facility.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I don't think you should've done that.|Obi-Wan Kenobi scolds Anakin Skywalker for interrupting Tuzes-Adaz's son's initiation ritual|<ref name="CW22" />}}
Tuzes-Adaz was a [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="CSWEAdaz" /> who had multiple sons<ref name="Databanknote" /> and served as the [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] of the [[Rokrul]] [[City/Legends|village]]<ref name="CSWEAdaz" /> on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Koobi system|Nelvaan system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic]]. At some point during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="CW22" /> the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]]-aligned [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] established a [[Techno Union laboratory|facility]] on Nelvaan that [[Siphon generator|siphoned]] the planet's geothermal energy. The chieftain and her warriors interpreted the environmental changes as an illness befalling the [[Great Mother]], a religious [[Deity/Legends|deity]] that the villagers honored.<ref name="CSWEAdaz" /> The village's warriors went off in search of the cause of the drastic shift in climate, but none came home, all having been captured and [[Mutant|mutated]] by the Techno Union.<ref name="CW24">{{CW|24}}</ref>

[[File:ShamanBeethoven-SWCWv2.png|thumb|left|250px|Tuzes-Adaz brings the Jedi to the shaman Orvos.]]
Near the end of the Clone Wars, in the year [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Nelvaan|mission]] to [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].</ref> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] [[Battle of Nelvaan|landed]] on Nelvaan in search of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|leader]] of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. Tuzes-Adaz discovered the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] after they interrupted her [[Tuzes-Adaz's son|eldest son's]] initiation ritual<ref name="Databank">{{DB|species|nelvaanian|Nelvaanian}}</ref> by [[Death/Legends|killing]]<ref name="CW22" /> the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Horax/Legends|horax]]<ref name="DBhorax">{{DB|creature|horax|horax}}</ref> the young boy had been [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]].<ref name="CW22" /> Remembering a prophecy passed down from the village [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], [[Orvos]], claiming that an [[Holt Kazed|off-worlder]] would come to Nelvaan and restore the Great Mother to health, she refrained from killing the Jedi and decided to bring them back to Rockrul to meet with Orvos instead.<ref name="CSWEAdaz" />

Tuzes-Adaz's decision ultimately saved her people from the Techno Union, as Skywalker went on to destroy the Separatist facility and put Nelvaan back on the path to normalcy.<ref name="CSWEAdaz" /> He then reunited the women and children of Rokrul with their male warrior counterparts, though the chieftain was initially hesitant to welcome them back due to their mutated appearance. However, upon seeing a child approach the mutated warriors without fear, Tuzes-Adaz and the other females had a change of heart.<ref name="CW25">{{CW|25}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Apu!|A child embraces her mutated father|<ref name="CW25" />}}
[[File:HappyCryingDogfacePeople-SWCWv2.png|thumb|right|250px|The chieftain has a change of heart and welcomes back the mutated Nelvaanian warriors.]]
As chieftain, Tuzes-Adaz showed wisdom and restraint when faced with the choice of killing Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker or bringing them to Orvos, choosing the latter after remembering the shaman's prophecy.<ref name="CSWEAdaz" /> Upon seeing the mutated Rokrul warriors return home, Tuzes-Adaz was hesitant to approach them. However, after a young child showed no discrimination and approached the mutated men without fear, the chieftain, along with all the other women, quickly changed their attitude from one of shock to one of joy.<ref name="CW25" /> Similar to her fellow villagers, Tuzes-Adaz had [[Color/Legends|blue]] skin, black [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="CW22" />

==Equipment==
Tuzes-Adaz donned a long, brown sleeveless [[Robe/Legends|robe]] with red, white, and black trim along the cuffs and collar. She also wore a large tribal [[Shield (physical armor)/Legends|shield]] mask of the same colors with the design of a face drawn on the front. She carried a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] with the head of a [[Bird/Legends|birdlike]] creature on the top. She also wore bands around her wrists and neck.<ref name="CW22" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tuzes-Adaz made her first appearance in [[Chapter 22 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 22]] of the animated ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' microseries,<ref name="CW22" /> which aired on [[March 22]], [[2005]] on [[Cartoon Network]].<ref name="TV.com">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-xxii-370294|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 2, Episode 2: Chapter XXII}}</ref> The character was given the name "Tuzes-Adaz" for the first time in her entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]].<ref name="CSWEAdaz" />
